IRGC claims several attacks on US army facilities in Kuwait
articles.summary: IRGC claims several attacks on US army facilities in Kuwait The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) announced attacks targeting US military infrastructure in Kuwait.  In a statement, the IRGC said it targeted a large warehouse of military equipment, a Patriot missile defense system, and a hangar of American MQ9 drones at the Ali al-Salem Air Base.  It also carried out strikes on two helicopter hangars in Camp al-Adiri as well as targeted a telecommunications tower in the Gulf country.

articles.title: US warns citizens in the Middle East to 'exercise caution'
articles.summary: US warns citizens in the Middle East to 'exercise caution' The US Department of State has urged citizens to "exercise caution and heightened vigilance" amid the escalating conflict in the region.  "Worldwide Caution: Due to heightened tensions in the Middle East, the security environment remains complex with the potential for unforeseen escalation," the statement read. It instructed Americans to be prepared for "flight cancellations, periodic airspace closures, and potential travel disruptions", and cautioned that US facilities across the world may be targeted.

articles.title: US passes $1 trillion military spending bill
articles.summary: US passes $1 trillion military spending bill The US House of Representatives narrowly passed its version of a massive defence policy bill on Wednesday, despite Democrats' concerns about its huge price tag, the Iran war and a provision that would boost Pentagon ties to Israel. The fiscal 2027 National Defense Authorization Act, or NDAA, authorises an unprecedented $1.15 trillion in military spending and passed the House by a vote of 216 to 212. articles.title: Vessel catches fire after passing through mined route in Hormuz Strait, IRGC says
articles.summary: Vessel catches fire after passing through mined route in Hormuz Strait, IRGC says Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said hat one of three oil tankers were set ablaze after a blast while attempting to pass through a mined route south of the Strait of Hormuz. According to the statement, the two other tankers turned back following this incident. "The Strait of Hormuz is under our control," the IRGC said, adding that as long as the US continues "its evil deeds" in the region, the waterway will remain closed.

articles.title: Fire onboard Saudi vessel hit in Red Sea
articles.summary: Fire onboard Saudi vessel hit in Red Sea  A Saudi-owned vessel was hit while sailing in the Red Sea, causing the front of the ship to be set on ifire, Saudi state news agency SPA reported citing an official source at the General Transport Authority. According to the source, all crew members onboard the Encelia were safe. Authorities were reportedly taking key measures to secure the vessel and crew as well as protect the marine environment.
